title: Using genomics to track environmental virus transmission and evolution

summary.hook: This poster describes three applications of unbiased metagenomic sequencing and bioinformatics to detect viruses and infer evolutionary history and transmission routes across...
summary.summary_30s: This poster describes three applications of unbiased metagenomic sequencing and bioinformatics to detect viruses and infer evolutionary history and transmission routes across environmental matrices, including mosquito pools, dust/air samples, and dust accumulation on electronic devices.

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This poster describes three applications of unbiased metagenomic sequencing and bioinformatics to detect viruses and infer evolutionary history and transmission routes...
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Methods / surveillance / environmental metagenomics (multi-application project; some experiments ongoing).. Not stated on poster for number of pools/samples; mosquito pool size is...
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: Metagenomics \+ bioinformatics can track viruses across dust/air and mosquito matrices; viral genomes can inform evolutionary history and transmission routes.
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: Environmental surveillance across vectors and dust/air could enhance situational awareness of viral circulation and potential transmission routes.
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: What does “unbiased metagenomics sequencing” mean in your workflow (shotgun sequencing vs targeted enrichment)?

key_numbers: Not stated on poster for number of pools/samples; mosquito pool size is stated (10–50).
Mosquito pools (10–50 mosquitoes per pool sample).
Mosquito pools (10–50 per pool) show virus-family patterns that vary by mosquito species (heatmap uses reads >1000 nt).
Dust can carry pathogens; small dust particles (≤10 microns) can reach the lower respiratory tract.
Treemap compares kingdom-level composition (eukaryote/bacteria/virus) across garden, vacuum, and passive air sampler...
takeaways: Metagenomics + bioinformatics can track viruses across dust/air and mosquito matrices; viral genomes can inform evolutionary history and transmission routes.
Mosquitoes are vectors for serious human viruses (examples: West Nile virus, Zika virus); poster states “There is currently no vaccine and no effective treatments.” (no...
Mosquito pools show virus-family patterns that vary by mosquito species (heatmap with colored boxes highlighting patterns).
